title: NumPy Tutorial: Stop Looping, Start Thinking in Arrays
description:
Python loops are 50–200x slower than NumPy on numerical data — and the fix isn't just a performance tweak, it's a completely different way of writing code.

In this tutorial I break down NumPy from first principles: what the ndarray actually is, why it outperforms Python lists, and the vectorized thinking that makes Pandas and scikit-learn click from first contact.

This is Tutorial 3 in the Python for Data Science series, designed for people who know Python basics and want to move toward real data work.

What you'll learn:
— The performance difference between Python loops and NumPy (with benchmarks)
— Creating arrays with np.array, np.zeros, np.arange, and np.linspace
— Vectorized operations and boolean indexing — the exact pattern Pandas uses for filtering
— Aggregations: mean, std, percentile, and IQR-based outlier detection in 6 lines
— 2D arrays, axis parameters, and row vs column operations
— The mental shift from step-by-step instructions to describing what you want

[TIMESTAMPS_PLACEHOLDER]

📋 Free worksheet — Python For Data Science Tutorial 310:
https://worksheets-thebreathnetwork.vercel.app/get-worksheet?slug=python-for-data-science-tutorial-310

If you're following the series, Tutorial 4 covers Matplotlib — visualization built on the same NumPy arrays you're learning here.
thumbnail_concept: Split screen: left side shows a Python for-loop with a spinning loading indicator and '12 seconds' label in red; right side shows a single NumPy line returning instantly with '0.08s' in green. Dark terminal background. Bold text overlay across top: 'STOP LOOPING'. Clean, high contrast, no clutter.
end_screen_suggestion: Python for Data Science Tutorial 4 — Matplotlib and Data Visualization
